author: sproul
content:
I don't remember agreeing to EIP-8045 conclusively, but maybe I missed the memo.

I am strongly against monkeying with the PTC or the attestation committees, and especially changing one (PTC) without changing the other (attestations), because in Lighthouse's caches we store them together (they have the same lookahead and can be calculated from the same state currently).

In general I'm really hesitant about changing any of these properties, because we've been burnt before (at Fulu) by subtle assumptions baked into our caches. To me it seems like the risk-reward ratio is heavily skewed against these sorts of changes being desirable to make.

I could be convinced to implement proposer-only EIP-8045 if the consensus is there, but it will slow us down.

Just my 2c

author: jtraglia
content: We have not yet agreed to 8045. And as it is currently spec’d, it only affects the proposer lookahead, not the attestation committees or PTCs.

author: sproul
content: Ok cool, just the talk of alpha9 made me think it was already in

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah that’s a fair point. I was thinking we might agree to it at ACDT, but we might need to wait for ACDC. Not sure.

author: barnabasbusa
content: Some ppl wanted to see what it would take to implement and it seems very easy for prysm and lodestar, and potuz is now pushing to get it included

author: sproul
content: I had a bit more of a look and I think proposer-only 8045 is not too bad for us, because it's constrained to the proposer_lookahead field. Would still prefer not to touch PTC or attester duties

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah we had multiple discussions about not touching any attestation duties. I feel like we should still try to do proposal changes and leave arrestee duties for another fork (or never).

author: barnabasbusa
content: also for touching the attestation duties we need to get potuz to retire first https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1509921285872816259/1509926417842962703

content:
